title: Introduction to Arrays
subject: DSA
description: Covers array basics including declaration initialization traversal and common operations. Teaches 1D and 2D arrays with hands-on examples for beginners.

title: Array Rotation and Reversal Techniques
subject: DSA
description: Explains left and right rotation of arrays reversal algorithms and in-place manipulation. Focuses on time complexity analysis of each approach.

title: Sliding Window Technique on Arrays
subject: DSA
description: Teaches the sliding window pattern for solving subarray problems including maximum sum subarray and fixed window problems with step-by-step walkthroughs.

title: Two Pointer Approach for Arrays
subject: DSA
description: Covers the two-pointer technique for problems like pair sum sorted array merging and removing duplicates. Explains optimal time and space complexity.

title: Kadane's Algorithm - Maximum Subarray Sum
subject: DSA
description: Detailed walkthrough of Kadane's algorithm for finding the maximum sum contiguous subarray. Includes proof of correctness and edge case handling.

title: Introduction to Linked Lists
subject: DSA
description: Introduces singly linked lists with node structure creation insertion and deletion. Compares linked lists with arrays and discusses memory allocation.

title: Doubly Linked List Operations
subject: DSA
description: Covers doubly linked list creation bidirectional traversal insertion at head tail and middle and deletion. Explains pointer manipulation in detail.

title: Circular Linked List Concepts
subject: DSA
description: Explains circular linked lists with both singly and doubly circular variants. Covers traversal insertion and deletion with real-world use cases like round-robin scheduling.

title: Linked List Reversal Techniques
subject: DSA
description: Teaches iterative and recursive reversal of linked lists. Includes reversing in groups K-reversal and partial reversal with complexity analysis.

title: Detecting and Removing Cycles in Linked Lists
subject: DSA
description: Covers Floyd's cycle detection algorithm for linked lists finding the start of the loop removing the cycle and proving the algorithm mathematically.

title: Introduction to Stacks
subject: DSA
description: Introduces stack data structure with LIFO principle push pop peek operations and implementations using arrays and linked lists for beginners.

title: Stack Applications - Balanced Parentheses and Expression Evaluation
subject: DSA
description: Covers practical stack applications including balanced bracket checking infix to postfix conversion and postfix expression evaluation with examples.

title: Monotonic Stack Problems
subject: DSA
description: Teaches monotonic stack patterns for problems like next greater element stock span problem largest rectangle in histogram with optimal O(n) solutions.

title: Introduction to Queues
subject: DSA
description: Covers queue fundamentals with FIFO principle enqueue dequeue front rear operations and implementations using arrays and circular arrays.

title: Deque and Priority Queue
subject: DSA
description: Explains double-ended queue and priority queue concepts with heaps. Covers applications in sliding window maximum problem and task scheduling.

title: Queue Using Stacks and Stack Using Queues
subject: DSA
description: Teaches how to implement a queue using two stacks and vice versa with amortized complexity analysis and comparison of both approaches.

title: Recursion Basics and Base Cases
subject: DSA
description: Explains recursion fundamentals recursive functions base conditions recursive calls and the call stack. Covers factorial Fibonacci and power examples.

title: Recursion vs Iteration - When to Use What
subject: DSA
description: Compares recursive and iterative solutions in terms of readability space complexity and performance. Uses sorting tree traversal and Fibonacci as examples.

title: Recursion Practice Problems - Backtracking Introduction
subject: DSA
description: Covers recursion-based problems including string permutations subsets and introduces backtracking for constraint satisfaction problems.

title: Binary Tree Introduction and Traversals
subject: DSA
description: Introduces binary tree node structure with inorder preorder and postorder traversals both recursive and iterative. Covers level order traversal using queues.

title: Binary Search Tree - Insertion Search and Deletion
subject: DSA
description: Explains BST properties insertion search and deletion operations with detailed case analysis for deletion including leaf node one-child and two-child scenarios.

title: BST Validation and Balancing Concepts
subject: DSA
description: Covers how to validate a BST using inorder traversal and range checking. Introduces AVL tree rotations and height-balanced BST properties.

title: Height Diameter and LCA in Binary Trees
subject: DSA
description: Teaches how to find height diameter and lowest common ancestor of binary trees with recursive and iterative approaches and complexity analysis.

title: BFS in Graphs - Level Order Graph Traversal
subject: DSA
description: Introduces BFS algorithm for graphs using adjacency list and queue. Covers multi-source BFS shortest path in unweighted graphs and level-by-level exploration.

title: DFS in Graphs - Depth First Search
subject: DSA
description: Explains DFS algorithm recursively and iteratively for graph traversal. Covers visited tracking component counting and DFS tree structure.

title: Shortest Path Algorithms - Dijkstra
subject: DSA
description: Covers Dijkstra's algorithm for single-source shortest path in weighted graphs with priority queue implementation time complexity and limitations.

title: Shortest Path - Bellman Ford and Floyd Warshall
subject: DSA
description: Explains Bellman-Ford for graphs with negative edges and Floyd-Warshall for all-pairs shortest paths with dynamic programming formulation and complexity analysis.

title: Topological Sort and DAG Applications
subject: DSA
description: Teaches topological sorting using DFS-based approach and Kahn's BFS algorithm. Covers applications in build systems course scheduling and dependency resolution.

title: Cycle Detection in Directed and Undirected Graphs
subject: DSA
description: Explains cycle detection techniques using DFS with color marking for directed graphs and union-find or DFS for undirected graphs with practical examples.

title: Minimum Spanning Tree - Prim and Kruskal
subject: DSA
description: Covers Prim's and Kruskal's algorithms for finding MST. Explains Union-Find data structure for Kruskal and greedy selection in Prim with complexity proofs.

title: Bubble Sort and Selection Sort
subject: DSA
description: Explains bubble sort and selection sort algorithms with step-by-step visualization. Covers time O(n2) and space O(1) complexity and stability analysis.

title: Merge Sort Algorithm
subject: DSA
description: In-depth walkthrough of merge sort with divide and conquer approach. Covers merging step recursive split time O(n log n) and space complexity with examples.

title: Quick Sort and Partition Techniques
subject: DSA
description: Teaches QuickSort with Lomuto and Hoare partition schemes. Covers best average and worst case analysis randomized pivot and practical performance tips.

title: Linear Search and Binary Search
subject: DSA
description: Covers linear and binary search algorithms with conditions for binary search sorted arrays iterative and recursive implementations and time complexity comparison.

title: Binary Search on Answer - Advanced Searching
subject: DSA
description: Teaches binary search on the answer space for optimization problems like minimum days peak element and search in rotated sorted array with generalized templates.

title: ER Model - Entity Relationship Diagram Basics
subject: DBMS
description: Covers ER model fundamentals including entities attributes relationships cardinality and participation constraints. Teaches how to design ER diagrams for real-world problems.

title: ER to Relational Schema Conversion
subject: DBMS
description: Explains the systematic conversion of ER diagrams into relational schemas covering strong and weak entities relationships and multivalued attributes.

title: Extended ER Model - Generalization Specialization
subject: DBMS
description: Covers enhanced ER concepts including generalization specialization aggregation and their representation in schemas with examples from university and company databases.

title: SQL Basics - DDL and DML Commands
subject: DBMS
description: Introduces SQL with CREATE ALTER DROP TABLE commands and INSERT UPDATE DELETE SELECT operations. Covers data types constraints and basic query structure.

title: SQL SELECT Queries and Filtering
subject: DBMS
description: Covers SELECT with WHERE LIKE BETWEEN IN IS NULL clauses ORDER BY GROUP BY and HAVING. Teaches writing effective filter queries with practical examples.

title: SQL Joins - Inner Outer Left Right
subject: DBMS
description: Explains INNER JOIN LEFT JOIN RIGHT JOIN FULL OUTER JOIN and CROSS JOIN with Venn diagram visualization and multiple real-world query examples.

title: SQL Aggregation Functions and GROUP BY
subject: DBMS
description: Teaches COUNT SUM AVG MIN MAX aggregate functions with GROUP BY and HAVING clauses. Covers nested aggregations and filtering on grouped results.

title: Advanced SQL - Subqueries and Views
subject: DBMS
description: Covers correlated and non-correlated subqueries inline views materialized views and WITH clause CTEs. Explains when to use subqueries versus joins for performance.

title: SQL Window Functions and Ranking
subject: DBMS
description: Teaches SQL window functions including ROW_NUMBER RANK DENSE_RANK LEAD LAG and PARTITION BY for analytical queries and reporting use cases.

title: Database Normalization - 1NF 2NF 3NF
subject: DBMS
description: Explains normalization concepts with functional dependencies. Covers 1NF 2NF and 3NF with step-by-step examples of decomposing unnormalized tables.

title: BCNF and Higher Normal Forms
subject: DBMS
description: Covers Boyce-Codd Normal Form 4NF and 5NF with examples. Explains when BCNF decomposition may lose dependency preservation and how to handle it.

title: Functional Dependencies and Closure
subject: DBMS
description: Teaches functional dependencies attribute closure canonical cover and minimal cover. Covers Armstrong's axioms and how to find all valid FDs from given dependencies.

title: Database Transactions and ACID Properties
subject: DBMS
description: Introduces database transactions with ACID properties atomicity consistency isolation and durability. Covers COMMIT ROLLBACK SAVEPOINT with banking transfer examples.

title: Concurrency Control - Locks and Protocols
subject: DBMS
description: Explains concurrency problems like dirty read lost update and phantom reads. Covers shared exclusive locks two-phase locking protocol and deadlock handling.

title: Transaction Isolation Levels
subject: DBMS
description: Covers READ UNCOMMITTED READ COMMITTED REPEATABLE READ and SERIALIZABLE isolation levels. Explains which anomalies each level prevents with practical database examples.

title: Database Indexing - Concepts and Types
subject: DBMS
description: Introduces database indexing with dense and sparse indexes ordered indexes and hash indexes. Explains how indexes speed up queries and their overhead on write operations.

title: B-Tree and B+ Tree Indexing
subject: DBMS
description: Covers B-Tree and B+ Tree structure for database indexing with insertion deletion and search operations. Explains why B+ Trees are preferred in RDBMS.

title: Clustered vs Non-Clustered Index and Query Optimization
subject: DBMS
description: Explains clustered and non-clustered indexes composite indexes and covering indexes. Covers query plan analysis EXPLAIN output and when to add or avoid indexes.

title: Hashing in DBMS - Static and Dynamic Hashing
subject: DBMS
description: Covers hashing for file organization including static hashing collision handling linear probing and dynamic hashing with extendible and linear hashing techniques.

title: File Organization in DBMS
subject: DBMS
description: Teaches heap sequential hash and clustered file organization methods. Compares access patterns insertion cost and suitable use cases for each file organization type.

title: Introduction to Operating Systems and System Calls
subject: OS
description: Introduces OS concepts including kernel vs user mode system calls and OS services. Covers types of operating systems and the role of OS in resource management.

title: Process Management and PCB
subject: OS
description: Covers process lifecycle states PCB structure context switching and process creation with fork. Explains inter-process communication mechanisms like pipes and shared memory.

title: Threads vs Processes - Multithreading
subject: OS
description: Explains the difference between processes and threads with shared memory model user-level vs kernel-level threads and advantages of multithreading for performance.

title: Thread Synchronization - Mutex and Semaphores
subject: OS
description: Teaches thread synchronization problems including race conditions critical section mutex locks and semaphores. Covers the producer-consumer and readers-writers problems.

title: CPU Scheduling Algorithms - FCFS SJF Round Robin
subject: OS
description: Covers major CPU scheduling algorithms including FCFS SJF SRTF and Round Robin with Gantt charts waiting time turnaround time calculations and comparison.

title: Priority Scheduling and Multilevel Queue Scheduling
subject: OS
description: Explains priority-based scheduling with preemptive and non-preemptive variants starvation and aging. Covers multilevel queue and multilevel feedback queue scheduling.

title: Deadlock Detection Prevention and Avoidance
subject: OS
description: Covers the four necessary conditions for deadlock detection algorithms resource allocation graph and banker's algorithm for deadlock avoidance with examples.

title: Deadlock Recovery and Handling Strategies
subject: OS
description: Explains deadlock recovery through process termination and resource preemption. Compares all four deadlock handling strategies with trade-offs and practical OS usage.

title: Memory Management - Paging and Segmentation
subject: OS
description: Covers paging mechanism with page tables TLB address translation and segmentation. Explains logical vs physical address space and memory protection techniques.

title: Virtual Memory and Page Replacement Algorithms
subject: OS
description: Teaches virtual memory concepts demand paging page faults and page replacement algorithms including FIFO LRU LFU Optimal with Belady's anomaly and performance analysis.

title: Memory Allocation Strategies - Fixed and Dynamic Partitioning
subject: OS
description: Covers fixed and dynamic memory partitioning strategies first fit best fit and worst fit algorithms internal vs external fragmentation and compaction techniques.

title: Cache Memory and Memory Hierarchy
subject: OS
description: Explains memory hierarchy from registers to cache RAM and disk. Covers cache levels hit ratio locality of reference and impact of cache on program performance.

title: File Systems and Disk Scheduling
subject: OS
description: Covers file system structure directory organization inode FAT NTFS and disk scheduling algorithms FCFS SSTF SCAN C-SCAN with seek time calculations.

title: OSI Model - Seven Layers Explained
subject: CN
description: Explains all seven layers of the OSI model with functions protocols and data units at each layer. Uses real-world analogies and packet flow examples to clarify layer interactions.

title: OSI vs TCP/IP Model Comparison
subject: CN
description: Compares OSI and TCP/IP reference models layer by layer. Covers why TCP/IP is the practical standard and how OSI layers map to TCP/IP layers with protocol examples.

title: Physical and Data Link Layer Protocols
subject: CN
description: Covers physical layer encoding and transmission and data link layer framing error detection CRC MAC addressing and flow control protocols like Stop-and-Wait.

title: TCP/IP Protocol Suite Overview
subject: CN
description: Introduces the TCP/IP protocol stack with Ethernet IP TCP UDP and application layer protocols. Explains how data flows through layers during internet communication.

title: TCP vs UDP - Comparison and Use Cases
subject: CN
description: Explains key differences between TCP and UDP protocols. Covers connection establishment three-way handshake reliability headers and when to choose TCP vs UDP.

title: TCP Congestion Control and Flow Control
subject: CN
description: Teaches TCP flow control using sliding window and congestion control mechanisms including slow start congestion avoidance fast retransmit and fast recovery algorithms.

title: IP Addressing and Subnetting
subject: CN
description: Covers IPv4 addressing classes subnetting with CIDR notation subnet masks network and host identification. Includes practice problems for subnetting and VLSM.

title: IPv6 - Features and Transition Mechanisms
subject: CN
description: Explains IPv6 address format 128-bit addressing header simplification auto-configuration and comparison with IPv4. Covers dual-stack tunneling and NAT64 transition strategies.

title: Routing Algorithms - RIP OSPF and BGP
subject: CN
description: Covers distance vector routing with RIP link state routing with OSPF and path vector BGP. Explains convergence loop prevention metrics and when each protocol is used.

title: Static and Dynamic Routing Explained
subject: CN
description: Explains static routing configuration advantages and use cases versus dynamic routing protocols. Covers routing table structure routing metrics and default route configuration.

title: NAT and IP Masquerading
subject: CN
description: Teaches Network Address Translation with static NAT dynamic NAT and PAT. Covers how NAT enables private IP ranges to access the internet and its impact on routing.

title: DNS - Domain Name System Explained
subject: CN
description: Covers how DNS translates domain names to IP addresses. Explains recursive and iterative resolution DNS record types (A MX CNAME) caching and DNS hierarchy.

title: DNS Security and DNSSEC
subject: CN
description: Explains DNS vulnerabilities like cache poisoning and DNS spoofing. Covers DNSSEC digital signatures zone signing and deployment challenges for secure DNS resolution.

title: HTTP and HTTPS Protocols
subject: CN
description: Covers HTTP request-response cycle methods GET POST PUT DELETE status codes headers and cookies. Explains HTTPS TLS handshake and certificate validation for secure communication.

title: HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 - Modern Web Protocols
subject: CN
description: Explains HTTP/2 features like multiplexing header compression and server push. Covers HTTP/3 built on QUIC protocol and performance improvements for modern web applications.

title: Classes and Objects in OOP
subject: OOP
description: Introduces classes and objects as blueprints and instances. Covers attributes methods constructors and the difference between class and instance variables with examples in Java and C++.

title: Constructors Destructors and this Keyword
subject: OOP
description: Covers default parameterized and copy constructors destructors and the this pointer. Explains constructor chaining and initialization lists with memory management implications.

title: Access Modifiers and Encapsulation
subject: OOP
description: Teaches encapsulation using private protected and public access modifiers. Covers getter and setter methods data hiding and why encapsulation is essential for maintainable code.

title: Static Members and Nested Classes
subject: OOP
description: Explains static variables and static methods shared across all instances. Covers nested classes inner classes and anonymous classes with practical design pattern examples.

title: Single and Multilevel Inheritance
subject: OOP
description: Covers inheritance basics IS-A relationship single inheritance multilevel inheritance and constructor calling order. Explains how derived classes reuse and extend base class behavior.

title: Multiple and Hybrid Inheritance
subject: OOP
description: Explains multiple inheritance with the diamond problem and resolution using virtual inheritance in C++ or interfaces in Java. Covers hybrid inheritance and ambiguity resolution.

title: Method Overriding and super Keyword
subject: OOP
description: Covers method overriding rules covariant return types and the super keyword for calling parent class methods and constructors with practical code examples.

title: Polymorphism - Compile Time and Runtime
subject: OOP
description: Explains compile-time polymorphism through method overloading and operator overloading and runtime polymorphism through method overriding and virtual functions with vtable internals.

title: Abstract Classes and Interfaces
subject: OOP
description: Covers abstract classes with abstract methods and interfaces with multiple implementation. Explains the difference between abstract classes and interfaces and when to use each.

title: Abstraction and Real-World Design Examples
subject: OOP
description: Introduces abstraction as hiding implementation complexity. Uses real-world analogies and design examples to show how abstraction simplifies interaction with complex systems.

title: Design Principles - SOLID in OOP
subject: OOP
description: Covers all five SOLID principles with code examples showing violations and fixes. Teaches how to design maintainable extensible and loosely coupled object-oriented systems.

title: OOP Relationships - Association Aggregation Composition
subject: OOP
description: Explains HAS-A relationships through association aggregation and composition. Covers UML class diagram notation lifecycle dependencies and when to prefer composition over inheritance.

title: Inter-Process Communication - Pipes Signals Sockets
subject: OS
description: Covers IPC mechanisms including unnamed and named pipes signals shared memory message queues and sockets. Explains synchronization challenges in IPC with producer-consumer examples.

title: OS Boot Process and Kernel Architecture
subject: OS
description: Explains the OS boot sequence from BIOS to kernel loading. Covers monolithic vs microkernel architecture system initialization process 0 and kernel space vs user space boundaries.

title: Operator Overloading in OOP
subject: OOP
description: Teaches operator overloading concepts in C++ and its equivalents in other languages. Covers overloading arithmetic comparison and stream operators with rules and restrictions.

title: OOP Design Patterns - Singleton Factory and Observer
subject: OOP
description: Covers three essential design patterns Singleton for single instance control Factory for object creation and Observer for event-driven programming with implementation in Java and Python.

title: Object Cloning and Shallow vs Deep Copy
subject: OOP
description: Explains object cloning mechanisms shallow copy vs deep copy. Covers copy constructors clone method in Java assignment operator overloading and memory implications of each approach.
